name: crime-and-punishment
description: Fyodor Dostoevsky's classic novel — exposing the psychological architecture of rationalizing evil, self-deception, and the path of redemption through suffering, confession, and love.
useCase: Identifying moral rationalization, understanding self-deception, deciding when and how to confess, transforming through suffering, recognizing self-destructive doubles.
knownRisks: The novel presents a deeply Christian framework; readers of other traditions may need to translate the concepts
Raskolnikov's story is extreme; everyday ethical lapses may not require this level of analysis
The 'extraordinary man' theory can be misinterpreted as endorsement rather than critique
